From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Australian musical group

| Bughouse | |
| --- | --- |
| [Bughouse 1990. Left to right Steve Campbell, Genevieve Maynard, Peter Brookes, Lea Cameron.](/wiki/File:Bughouse_1990.jpg "Bughouse 1990. Left to right Steve Campbell, Genevieve Maynard, Peter Brookes, Lea Cameron.") Bughouse 1990. Left to right Steve Campbell, Genevieve Maynard, Peter Brookes, Lea Cameron. | |
| Background information | |
| Origin | [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), Australia |
| Genres | [Indie rock](/wiki/Indie_rock "Indie rock") |
| Years active | 1989–1994 |
| Labels | Ursula, [Mushroom](/wiki/Mushroom_Records "Mushroom Records") |
|  | |
| Past members | Peter Brookes Steve Campbell Lea Cameron Genevieve Maynard Nick Fisher |

**Bughouse** were an [independent](/wiki/Independent_music "Independent music") band from [Sydney](/wiki/Sydney "Sydney"), who toured the East Coast of Australia from 1989 to 1994.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bughouse_(band)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

### 1989–1991: Formation and early period

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bughouse_(band)&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: 1989–1991: Formation and early period")]

Formed by drummer Peter Brookes in 1989 as a recording project, the original line-up was completed by Lea Cameron (vocals), Steve Campbell (guitar) and Genevieve Maynard (bass).[[1]](#cite_note-1)[*[unreliable source?](/wiki/Wikipedia:Reliable_sources "Wikipedia:Reliable sources")*][[2]](#cite_note-2) Their first show was on Saturday 27 May 1989 at the [Hopetoun Hotel](/wiki/Hopetoun_Hotel "Hopetoun Hotel"), [Surry Hills](/wiki/Surry_Hills "Surry Hills"). In their first six months Bughouse played around Sydney, predominantly at the Hopetoun, Annandale, Lansdowne and [Sandringham](/wiki/Sandringham_Hotel,_Newtown "Sandringham Hotel, Newtown") hotels, and at various university campuses.

Their debut single "V for Vendetta"/"Burn it Back" was produced by [Damien Lovelock](/wiki/Damien_Lovelock "Damien Lovelock"), lead singer of [The Celibate Rifles](/wiki/The_Celibate_Rifles "The Celibate Rifles")[[3]](#cite_note-3) and featured [Louis Tillett](/wiki/Louis_Tillett "Louis Tillett") of the Wet Taxis on piano.[[4]](#cite_note-4) "V for Vendetta" was released on the band's own Ursula label, receiving high rotation on radio station [Triple J](/wiki/Triple_J "Triple J")[[5]](#cite_note-5) and reaching number six[[6]](#cite_note-6) on the Australian Independent Charts. [ABC TV](/wiki/ABC_(Australian_TV_channel) "ABC (Australian TV channel)") made a video of the song for an "undiscovered" section on weekly music show *[Rage](/wiki/Rage_(TV_program) "Rage (TV program)")*. The band recorded their first [Live at the Wireless](/wiki/Live_at_the_Wireless "Live at the Wireless") for Triple J at ABC Studio 1 in [Darlinghurst](/wiki/Darlinghurst "Darlinghurst").

Bughouse returned to the studio some months later to record the *Bughouse* EP with engineer John Hresc at Powerhouse Studios.[[7]](#cite_note-7) The EP included four tracks – "Tax Stamp", "Bruce's Song", "Salad Days" and "One More Thing".[[8]](#cite_note-8) The EP reached number four on the Australian Independent Charts[[9]](#cite_note-9) These two first records showcased the band's assured and eclectic sound.[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)

After this early success, the band were courted by several major labels eventually signing a record deal with [Mushroom Records](/wiki/Mushroom_Records "Mushroom Records")' White Label.[[12]](#cite_note-12)

The band's debut album, *Every Fool in Town* was recorded over ten days again with Lovelock producing and Tony Espie[[13]](#cite_note-13) engineering. It included guest performances by [Amanda Brown](/wiki/Amanda_Brown_(musician) "Amanda Brown (musician)") of the [Go-Betweens](/wiki/The_Go-Betweens "The Go-Betweens")[[14]](#cite_note-14) and [James Cruickshank](/wiki/James_Cruickshank "James Cruickshank") of the [Cruel Sea](/wiki/The_Cruel_Sea_(band) "The Cruel Sea (band)"). The album received positive reviews,[[15]](#cite_note-15)[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17) and reached number 1 on the Australian Independent Charts.[[18]](#cite_note-18) A video was made for the single[[19]](#cite_note-19) "Hell for Leather".

### 1992–1994 Bardo and Fink Tank

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bughouse_(band)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: 1992–1994 Bardo and Fink Tank")]

For the rest of their career, Bughouse toured extensively around Australia, playing shows with many notable local and international bands, including [Crowded House](/wiki/Crowded_House "Crowded House"),[[20]](#cite_note-20) the [Hoodoo Gurus](/wiki/Hoodoo_Gurus "Hoodoo Gurus"), [Hunters & Collectors](/wiki/Hunters_%26_Collectors "Hunters & Collectors"), [Yothu Yindi](/wiki/Yothu_Yindi "Yothu Yindi"),[[21]](#cite_note-21) [Concrete Blonde](/wiki/Concrete_Blonde "Concrete Blonde")[[22]](#cite_note-22) and [Bob Dylan](/wiki/Bob_Dylan "Bob Dylan").

During the period between *Every Fool in Town* and *Bardo*, Peter Brookes left the band and was replaced by Nick Fisher (Wet Taxis, [New Christs](/wiki/New_Christs "New Christs")).[[23]](#cite_note-23)

Bughouse recorded the EP *Bardo* in 1992.[[24]](#cite_note-24) The name of the EP came from the Tibetan Book of the Dead, and referred to the limbo the band had been in as a result of record company indecision in moving their recording schedule ahead. The EP contained five tracks, and a video was made for the song "Brightest Firework".

After parting ways with Mushroom Records, a final album *Fink Tank* was released through Mushroom Distribution Services in 1994 to strong reviews,[[25]](#cite_note-25) including by *[Rolling Stone](/wiki/Rolling_Stone "Rolling Stone")* magazine who described it as a "screaming surprise packet".[[26]](#cite_note-26) The album was recorded by [Tim Powles](/wiki/Tim_Powles "Tim Powles"), and mixed by [Paul McKercher](/wiki/Paul_McKercher "Paul McKercher"). The band released a video for the album's second track "Fathom" and also appeared on ABC TV's *[Live & Sweaty](/wiki/Live_%26_Sweaty "Live & Sweaty")*.[[27]](#cite_note-27)

Their final performance was on 4 October 1994 at the Annandale Hotel, Sydney.

## Discography

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bughouse_(band)&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Discography")]

### Albums and EPs

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Bughouse_(band)&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Albums and EPs")]

| Release | Year | Label | type |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| *Bughouse* | 1989 | Ursula | EP |
| *Every Fool in Town* | 1990 | Ursula/Mushroom | Album |
| *Bardo* | 1992 | Ursula/MDS | EP |
| *Fink Tank* | 1994 | Ursula | Album |

### Singles

| Release | Label | type | Year |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| "V for Vendetta" | Ursula Records | Single | 1989 |
| "V for Vendetta" | Ursula/Waterfront | Re-released / single |
| "Somebody To Love" | Ursula/Mushroom | 12" single | 1990 |
